message:
Sync beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -69,10 +69,10 @@
 {"id":"bd-32m","title":"Fix cancel phase completion consistency in schedulers","description":"Scheduler and parallel scheduler complete cancelled tasks without always advancing cancel_phase to ASX_CANCEL_PHASE_COMPLETED. This can leave completed tasks reporting stale cancel phases (REQUESTED/CANCELLING/FINALIZING) and violate lifecycle invariants. Add fixes + regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayRiver","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:13:24.998271566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:19:38.156933062Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:19:38.156914558Z","close_reason":"Fixed cancel_phase completion consistency in scheduler+parallel and added regression tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-32t","title":"Fix overflow-prone load percentage math in runtime overload/adapters","description":"Deep audit found repeated load_pct/core_pct computations using (used * 100u) / capacity in runtime adapter/overload code. This can overflow uint32_t before division and misclassify overload states. Replace with widened uint64_t arithmetic and add/adjust tests to lock behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CodexAudit","created_at":"2026-03-01T04:52:51.530508769Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:01:02.979994774Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:01:02.979965178Z","close_reason":"Implemented safe load-percentage arithmetic with overflow saturation, fixed router capacity scaling edge cases, and added regression tests. Validated with rch: format-check, lint, build, test-unit, test, conformance.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3tx","title":"Honor BUILD_DIR in conformance/profile-parity artifact generation","description":"Conformance/profile-parity helper scripts partially hardcoded build paths, causing failures when invoking with alternate BUILD_DIR (especially under rch worker permission differences). Ensure run_conformance.sh forwards BUILD_DIR to make build and run_profile_parity.sh writes adapter artifact under selected BUILD_DIR.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GrayRiver","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:07:33.021827010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:07:35.858189947Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:07:35.858161123Z","close_reason":"Patched tools/ci/run_conformance.sh to pass BUILD_DIR to make build, and tools/ci/run_profile_parity.sh to write adapter artifact under selected BUILD_DIR.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-3vt","title":"EPIC: Advanced Alpha Levers and Research Backlog (No Feature Loss)","description":"Background:
The plan includes advanced ideas (formal assurance ladder, lock-free alternatives, cache-oblivious layouts, etc.). These should be pursued via controlled spikes with rollback and proof artifacts.

Goal:
Run high-EV research tasks that can elevate performance/assurance without semantic compromise.

Constraints:
- one lever at a time
- explicit baseline/profile/isomorphism artifacts
- fallback path mandatory

Why this serves users:
- keeps project ambitious without destabilizing the core delivery path.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.632150816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:41.462963973Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alpha","optimization","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:14:43.612984800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3vt","title":"EPIC: Advanced Alpha Levers and Research Backlog (No Feature Loss)","description":"Background:
The plan includes advanced ideas (formal assurance ladder, lock-free alternatives, cache-oblivious layouts, etc.). These should be pursued via controlled spikes with rollback and proof artifacts.

Goal:
Run high-EV research tasks that can elevate performance/assurance without semantic compromise.

Constraints:
- one lever at a time
- explicit baseline/profile/isomorphism artifacts
- fallback path mandatory

Why this serves users:
- keeps project ambitious without destabilizing the core delivery path.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.632150816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T21:06:53.850235463Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T21:06:53.850215876Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alpha","optimization","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:14:43.612984800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.1","title":"Pilot formal assurance ladder from golden outputs to bounded model checks to translation validation","description":"Background:
The plan calls for replacing missing compile-time guarantees with layered assurance artifacts.

Subtasks:
- Define staged assurance levels and entry/exit criteria.
- Pilot bounded model checks on selected lifecycle/state-machine components.
- Define lightweight translation-validation checks for critical transformations.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Produces concrete recommendation with cost/benefit and integration path.
- Includes fallback plan if tooling cost outweighs value.
- Leaves behind reusable templates for future formal efforts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.410198918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:50:56.417210097Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:50:56.417190430Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["assurance","formal","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.956578773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.861498197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.410198918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-3vt.10","title":"Prototype optional Rust-AST zero-drift extractor for transition and invariant artifacts","description":"Background:
Plan section 6.8.3B allows an optional zero-drift extractor path to reduce manual transcription drift from Rust semantics.

Subtasks:
- Build minimal tooling prototype (Rust AST parser under `tools/`) that emits transition X-macros and invariant schema fragments.
- Compare generated artifacts against manually curated baseline for one subsystem.
- Define promotion criteria, review requirements, and rollback plan if extractor proves brittle.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Prototype demonstrates measurable drift-reduction value on sampled subsystem.
- Generated artifacts pass parity review before any adoption.
- Clear go/no-go decision and risk note are recorded.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:24.858534052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:45:01.488065120Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","research","spec","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.206291901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-296.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.095517036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:24.858534052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-3vt.11","title":"Evaluate optional wasm32 determinism-oracle gate for host-coupling detection","description":"Background:
Plan section 10.7.4 defines a high-value optional wasm oracle to expose hidden host coupling and nondeterminism.

Subtasks:
- Add prototype build/run path for core profile under wasm sandbox target.
- Run selected parity fixtures and compare semantic digests against native deterministic mode.
- Document classes of defects this oracle catches and maintenance cost.
- Define criteria for optional ongoing CI inclusion vs on-demand diagnostic use.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Prototype demonstrates reproducible parity runs in sandbox mode.
- Value/cost decision is documented with evidence.
- If retained, invocation path and artifact expectations are documented.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:30.292788723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.407969762Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","determinism","portability","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.311600205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.11","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:30.292788723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.11","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.407922965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3vt.10","title":"Prototype optional Rust-AST zero-drift extractor for transition and invariant artifacts","description":"Background:
Plan section 6.8.3B allows an optional zero-drift extractor path to reduce manual transcription drift from Rust semantics.

Subtasks:
- Build minimal tooling prototype (Rust AST parser under `tools/`) that emits transition X-macros and invariant schema fragments.
- Compare generated artifacts against manually curated baseline for one subsystem.
- Define promotion criteria, review requirements, and rollback plan if extractor proves brittle.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Prototype demonstrates measurable drift-reduction value on sampled subsystem.
- Generated artifacts pass parity review before any adoption.
- Clear go/no-go decision and risk note are recorded.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:24.858534052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:49:24.000169440Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:49:24.000147309Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","research","spec","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.206291901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-296.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.095517036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:24.858534052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-3vt.11","title":"Evaluate optional wasm32 determinism-oracle gate for host-coupling detection","description":"Background:
Plan section 10.7.4 defines a high-value optional wasm oracle to expose hidden host coupling and nondeterminism.

Subtasks:
- Add prototype build/run path for core profile under wasm sandbox target.
- Run selected parity fixtures and compare semantic digests against native deterministic mode.
- Document classes of defects this oracle catches and maintenance cost.
- Define criteria for optional ongoing CI inclusion vs on-demand diagnostic use.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Prototype demonstrates reproducible parity runs in sandbox mode.
- Value/cost decision is documented with evidence.
- If retained, invocation path and artifact expectations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:30.292788723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T21:06:25.169960974Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T21:06:25.169935567Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","determinism","portability","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.311600205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.11","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:30.292788723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.11","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.407922965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.2","title":"Evaluate lock-free queue and shadow-path equivalence for high-contention messaging paths","description":"Background:
Lock-free fast paths may improve throughput/tail behavior, but only if semantic equivalence and rollback are guaranteed.

Subtasks:
- Implement spike version for candidate queue design.
- Compare against baseline queue under identical fixture/benchmark sets.
- Produce isomorphism and fallback artifacts per optimization-governance rules.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Decision made with measured EV and explicit risk profile.
- Semantic equivalence validated on shared fixture set.
- Safe fallback remains first-class and tested.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.497384328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T06:20:36.444583754Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T06:20:36.444558367Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lockfree","performance","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:35.136800448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:35.046812075Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.497384328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.3","title":"Study cache-oblivious or locality-aware arena layouts for scheduler hot paths","description":"Background:
Memory layout can materially impact performance across heterogeneous CPU/cache profiles.

Subtasks:
- Evaluate candidate layout strategies for region/task/obligation arenas.
- Benchmark across representative host and embedded targets.
- Document portability impact and maintenance tradeoffs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Clear go/no-go recommendation with evidence.
- No adoption without semantic neutrality proof.
- Includes rollback and guardrail strategy.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.587542890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:26:02.558190316Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:26:02.558170639Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","performance","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:35.317210887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:35.227015987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.587542890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.4","title":"Add memory-model litmus suite and cross-compiler codegen checks for critical atomic paths","description":"Background:
Cross-platform correctness for atomics paths needs targeted validation beyond unit tests.

Subtasks:
- Define litmus cases for critical synchronization assumptions.
- Run across compiler/target matrix and compare outcomes.
- Add codegen-diff checks for sensitive kernels where practical.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Litmus suite integrated into non-blocking research or gated checks as appropriate.
- Findings feed directly into profile/runtime design decisions.
- Any unsafe assumption is documented and mitigated.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.677548976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T05:56:59.612929767Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T05:56:59.612903859Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atomics","correctness","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.677548976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:35.419423318Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:35.506977880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
